# Lumen Launch Plan
### Smart Reading Lamp — $79 | Adaptive Color Temperature

---

## 1. Positioning

**Category:** Premium-accessible smart lighting for readers, focused users, and sleep-conscious buyers.

**Core promise:** *"Light that keeps time with you."* Lumen automatically shifts from crisp daylight for focus to warm amber for evening wind-down—protecting your eyes and your sleep without a single manual adjustment.

**Target segments:**
- Avid readers and students (long-session eye comfort)
- Remote workers upgrading home desks
- Wellness/sleep-hygiene buyers (circadian-aware lighting)

**Differentiation vs. competitors:**
| Lumen | Cheap LED lamps | Premium smart lighting ($150+) |
|-------|-----------------|-------------------------------|
| Auto circadian shift | Manual only | Yes, but app-heavy |
| $79 sweet spot | $20–40 | $150–300 |
| Set-and-forget simplicity | N/A | Requires ecosystem |

**Key message pillars:**
1. **Automatic** — no tuning, adjusts to time of day.
2. **Healthier** — reduces eye strain and blue-light exposure at night.
3. **Affordable premium** — smart lighting benefits without the ecosystem tax.

---

## 2. Launch Channels

**Owned**
- Product landing page with pre-order → launch conversion
- Email list (nurture sequence + launch-day blast)
- Existing social accounts (short-form demo video: day-to-night time-lapse)

**Earned**
- Reviewer/creator seeding: sleep, productivity, and home-tech niches (10–15 mid-tier creators)
- Press outreach to lifestyle/gadget outlets (embargoed for launch day)

**Paid**
- Meta/Instagram: carousel + video, retargeting page visitors
- Google Search: intent keywords ("reading lamp for eyes," "sleep-friendly lamp")
- Paid creator collaborations (2–3 higher-reach for launch week)

**Marketplace**
- Amazon listing live at launch (capture high-intent search)
- Optional early-bird discount ($69) for first 500 units to drive velocity/reviews

---

## 3. Two-Week Timeline

**Week 1 — Prime the market**
| Day | Action |
|-----|--------|
| 1 | Landing page live; email list warm-up ("something's coming") |
| 2 | Ship review units to creators/press under embargo |
| 3 | Publish teaser video; open pre-orders at early-bird price |
| 4 | Begin paid retargeting to page visitors |
| 5 | Mid-week email: feature deep-dive (circadian benefit) |
| 6–7 | Monitor pre-order data; brief creators, confirm launch-day posts |

**Week 2 — Launch and amplify**
| Day | Action |
|-----|--------|
| 8 | **LAUNCH.** Embargo lifts; press + creator content goes live; Amazon listing active; launch email blast |
| 9 | Turn on full paid spend (Meta + Google); reshare creator content |
| 10 | Feature customer/early reviews; social proof push |
| 11 | Address early questions/objections via FAQ + email |
| 12 | Mid-launch performance review; reallocate paid budget to winners |
| 13 | Urgency push: early-bird price ending soon |
| 14 | Early-bird ends; transition to $79; recap and retention email |

---

## 4. Top Three Risks & Mitigations

**Risk 1 — "Auto-adjust" perceived as gimmick / low differentiation.**
- *Mitigation:* Lead all messaging with the concrete benefit (eye strain, sleep) rather than the feature. Use side-by-side time-lapse demos so the value is instantly visible. Seed creators who can credibly speak to sleep/focus outcomes.

**Risk 2 — Inventory misalignment (stockout or overstock).**
- *Mitigation:* Use pre-order data (Days 3–7) as a demand signal before committing full launch spend. Cap early-bird at 500 units to protect margin and gauge velocity. Set clear reorder trigger thresholds; hold a paid-spend brake if inventory drops below 2-week cover.

**Risk 3 — Weak launch-day reviews undermine social proof.**
- *Mitigation:* Seed units early (Day 2) so genuine reviews exist by launch. Prioritize product QA on the one feature that must not fail (smooth, reliable color shift). Prepare a responsive support/FAQ flow to resolve issues fast and convert early feedback before it hits public reviews at scale.

---

**Success metrics to watch:** pre-order conversion rate, launch-week units sold, CAC by channel, Amazon review rating (target ≥4.3), and email → purchase rate.

# Lumen Launch Plan

**Product:** Lumen — $79 smart reading lamp with automatic color temperature adjustment (warm at night, cool during day)

---

## 1. Positioning

**Core promise:** *"Light that keeps time with your body."*

**Positioning statement:**
For focused readers and home professionals who care about sleep and eye comfort, Lumen is the reading lamp that automatically shifts color temperature to match your circadian rhythm — so you get bright focus by day and restful warmth by night, without touching a dial.

**Key differentiators:**
- **Automatic, not manual** — competitors require app fiddling; Lumen just works.
- **Wellness-anchored** — framed around sleep and eye health, not gadgetry.
- **Accessible premium** — $79 sits below smart-home flagships ($120+) but above commodity lamps ($30), signaling quality without luxury friction.

**Target segments (priority order):**
1. Knowledge workers / remote professionals (desk + evening use)
2. Avid readers (bedside use, sleep-conscious)
3. Wellness/biohacking-curious buyers (circadian-rhythm messaging resonates)

---

## 2. Launch Channels

| Channel | Role | Primary message |
|---|---|---|
| **Owned email list** | Warm launch, early conversion | Founder story + early-bird offer |
| **DTC website / landing page** | Conversion hub | Full benefit story, demo video |
| **Instagram + TikTok** | Awareness, demo | Time-lapse of light shifting; "day vs. night" comparison |
| **Micro-influencers (sleep, productivity, home office)** | Credibility + reach | Authentic desk/bedside placements |
| **PR / newsletters (Gear, wellness, home-tech)** | Third-party validation | "Best new circadian lighting" angle |
| **Paid social retargeting** | Recover abandoners | Reviews + limited launch pricing |

**Launch offer:** First 500 units at $69 (13% off) + free shipping to drive urgency and early reviews.

---

## 3. Two-Week Timeline

**Week 1 — Build-up**
- **Day 1–2:** Finalize landing page, demo video, and email sequence. Confirm inventory + fulfillment.
- **Day 3:** Send teaser email to list ("Something's coming"). Seed product to 8–10 influencers under embargo.
- **Day 4–5:** Publish 2–3 organic social teasers (mystery + demo clips). Brief PR contacts.
- **Day 6:** Warm-up email #2 with early-bird waitlist signup.
- **Day 7:** Internal go/no-go check — inventory, site load test, support scripts ready.

**Week 2 — Launch**
- **Day 8 (Launch Day):** Landing page live. Launch email to full list. Influencer posts go live simultaneously. PR embargo lifts.
- **Day 9:** Turn on paid social (awareness + retargeting). Amplify best-performing influencer content.
- **Day 10–11:** Push social proof — repost user unboxings, early reviews.
- **Day 12:** Mid-launch email: "500 units nearly gone" urgency push.
- **Day 13:** Optimize ads (cut losers, scale winners). Address FAQ objections in content.
- **Day 14:** "Last day for launch price" email + social. Recap metrics, plan sustained phase.

---

## 4. Top Three Risks & Mitigations

**Risk 1: "Why not just a $30 lamp?" — price justification fails.**
- *Mitigation:* Lead all messaging with the automatic circadian benefit and eye/sleep health, not features. Include a simple day/night demo video that makes the value instantly visible. Offer a 30-day money-back guarantee to de-risk the purchase.

**Risk 2: Inventory shortfall or fulfillment delays.**
- *Mitigation:* Cap the launch offer at a known stock quantity (500 units) so urgency is honest and not overselling. Set clear shipping expectations on the product page. Hold a small reserve buffer for influencer/PR replacements and defect swaps.

**Risk 3: Weak launch-day traction (low awareness).**
- *Mitigation:* Don't rely on launch day alone — build a waitlist during Week 1 to guarantee day-one demand. Diversify beyond paid ads with influencer and PR so success isn't tied to one channel. Keep ad budget flexible to scale into whatever channel shows early traction.

---

## Success Metrics
- Units sold in first 14 days (target: sell out 500-unit early batch)
- Landing page conversion rate (target: ≥3%)
- Email open/click rates and waitlist size pre-launch
- Cost per acquisition vs. $79 price (target CPA < $25)
- ≥15 authentic reviews collected for sustained-phase social proof

# Lumen Launch Plan

**Product:** Lumen — a $79 smart reading lamp that automatically adjusts color temperature to the time of day (cool/energizing by day, warm/relaxing by night).

---

## 1. Positioning

**One-liner:** *Lumen is the reading lamp that knows what time it is — cooler light to focus, warmer light to wind down, automatically.*

**Target audience:**
- **Primary:** Knowledge workers and avid readers (28–45) who care about sleep quality, focus, and home aesthetics.
- **Secondary:** Wellness-minded gift buyers; people setting up a home office or bedside reading nook.

**Category framing:** Position between "dumb" premium lamps ($40–60) and complex smart-home lighting systems ($120+). Lumen is *effortless wellness lighting* — no app required to get value.

**Key differentiators:**
1. **Automatic, no-fuss** — circadian adjustment out of the box; not another device to configure.
2. **Health/sleep angle** — warmer evening light supports natural wind-down.
3. **Price-value** — smart lighting benefit at a "single lamp" price point.

**Positioning statement:** *For readers and remote workers who want lighting that supports their focus and sleep, Lumen is a smart lamp that automatically matches its light to the time of day — unlike smart bulbs that require constant tinkering, Lumen just works.*

---

## 2. Launch Channels

| Channel | Role | Priority |
|---|---|---|
| **Owned email list** | Warm-up + launch-day conversion driver | High |
| **DTC website / landing page** | Conversion hub; clear "sleep + focus" story | High |
| **Instagram + TikTok (organic + paid)** | Visual demo of light shifting; aesthetic home content | High |
| **Micro-influencers** (home/wellness/productivity niches) | Credibility + reach; unboxing & real-use content | High |
| **PR / product blogs** (gadget, wellness, design outlets) | Third-party validation | Medium |
| **Reddit / niche communities** (r/homeoffice, r/sleep) | Authentic engagement, not hard sell | Medium |
| **Paid search** (branded + "smart reading lamp") | Capture high-intent buyers | Medium |

---

## 3. Two-Week Timeline

### Week 1 — Warm-up & Seed
- **Day 1–2:** Finalize landing page, product photography/video, and email sequence. Confirm inventory & fulfillment.
- **Day 2–3:** Ship review units to 8–12 micro-influencers and 3–5 press contacts (embargoed).
- **Day 3–4:** Publish teaser content ("Coming soon") on social + email; open early-access waitlist.
- **Day 5:** Post first influencer teaser content; begin building anticipation.
- **Day 6–7:** Warm-up email to list (the "why" story: light + sleep + focus). Seed community discussions.

### Week 2 — Launch & Amplify
- **Day 8 (Launch Day):** Site goes live; launch email to full list; press embargo lifts; influencers post reviews; paid social + search go live. Early-bird offer (e.g., free shipping or $10 off first 500).
- **Day 9–10:** Amplify best-performing content; retarget site visitors; share early testimonials/social proof.
- **Day 11–12:** Reddit/community AMA or founder post; second email (social proof + urgency on early-bird).
- **Day 13:** Address FAQs/objections publicly (returns, brightness, no-app clarity).
- **Day 14:** "Early-bird ending" email + close first offer; recap metrics and plan next wave.

---

## 4. Top Three Risks & Mitigations

**Risk 1 — "Why not just buy a smart bulb?" (differentiation confusion)**
- *Mitigation:* Lead all messaging with the *automatic, no-app, sleep-and-focus* benefit. Include a simple comparison graphic (Lumen vs. smart bulbs vs. regular lamps) on the landing page and in influencer briefs.

**Risk 2 — Wellness claims scrutiny / skepticism**
- *Mitigation:* Use careful language ("supports natural wind-down," "designed to align with your day") rather than medical claims. Cite general circadian-lighting principles, not unverified health outcomes. Have legal/compliance review copy before launch.

**Risk 3 — Inventory/fulfillment strain from a demand spike**
- *Mitigation:* Cap the early-bird offer (first 500 units) to control velocity; set clear stock counters and realistic ship dates on-site. Pre-confirm 3PL/fulfillment capacity and hold safety stock. Have a waitlist ready if units sell out.

---

**Success metrics to track:** landing page conversion rate, launch-day units sold, cost per acquisition (paid), email open/click rates, and influencer content engagement. Review at Day 14 to decide on the next promotional wave.

# Lumen Launch Plan
### Smart Reading Lamp · $79 · 14-Day Launch · 2-Person Team · $25,000 Budget

---

## 0. How to Read This Plan

This is a **validated-learning launch**, not a scaled revenue event. With two people, $25k, and 14 days, the realistic outcome is proving that Lumen's positioning converts and that its unit economics support future paid scaling — *not* a large one-time revenue spike.

Three unknowns can invalidate the channel mix. **These must be resolved on Day 0 before spend begins.** The plan branches accordingly.

| # | Must-answer question | Resolve by | What it changes |
|---|----|----|----|
| 1 | Gross margin per unit at $79 | Day 0 AM | Whether paid acquisition is viable at all |
| 2 | In-stock or pre-order? | Day 0 AM | Success metrics (revenue vs. deposits) + top risk |
| 3 | Email list size / social following / existing reviews? | Day 0 AM | Whether the cheapest channels are real |

**Default assumptions used to write the plan** (revise if Day 0 audit contradicts):
- Margin is thin-to-moderate (~$25–35/unit) → paid is a *gated test*, not the engine.
- Launch is **in-stock** → revenue-based success criteria apply. (Pre-order branch noted in §5.)
- A small owned list exists (≥1k emails). If zero, see the "No List" branch in §3.

---

## 1. Positioning

**One-line positioning:**
> *Lumen is the reading lamp that gives you bright, focused light for the day and warm, easy-on-the-eyes light at night — automatically, so your reading nook always feels right.*

**Why this framing:** The core differentiator — automatic color-temperature shift by time of day — is inherently a wellness/circadian benefit. But circadian health claims invite FTC substantiation risk and refund backlash. We therefore lead with **demonstrable, non-medical value** we can prove on camera:

| Lead with (safe + provable) | Avoid (unprovable / risky) |
|---|---|
| Visible warm→cool shift on video | "Improves sleep" / "regulates melatonin" |
| "No harsh blue light at night" | "Medically proven" / "doctor recommended" |
| Eye comfort during long reading | Specific health outcomes |
| Set-and-forget automation vs. fiddly Hue apps | Clinical circadian claims |

**Competitive wedge vs. Philips Hue / Yeelight ($40–70):** Those are *general* smart bulbs requiring app fiddling and scenes. Lumen is a **purpose-built reading lamp that just works automatically** — no scenes to build, no bulbs to buy, designed for one job. Verify before launch that competitors don't already auto-adjust out of the box; if they do, sharpen the wedge to design + zero-setup.

**Message-testing requirement:** Before scaling any paid spend, cheaply test 2–3 angles: (A) "automatic day-to-night," (B) "eye comfort for readers," (C) "no more harsh light at night." Scale only the winner.

---

## 2. Budget Allocation ($25,000)

| Line | Amount | % | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Influencer seeding (product + small fees) | $8,000 | 32% | Load-bearing channel |
| Paid social (gated test) | $6,000 | 24% | **Capped; released in stages** |
| Email/SMS tools + landing/creative | $2,500 | 10% | Klaviyo, landing page, video edit |
| Product units for seeding/reviews (~40 @ COGS) | $2,000 | 8% | ~40 units at cost |
| Contingency / margin buffer | $4,000 | 16% | Protects against CAC overrun |
| PR (opportunistic, tools + samples) | $1,500 | 6% | Best-effort, no committed target |
| Reserve (kept liquid to Day 7) | $1,000 | 4% | Released only if paid validates |
| **Total** | **$25,000** | 100% | |

**Paid spend is gated, not front-loaded:**
- Days 1–4: $1,500 test across 3 message angles.
- **Day 5 gate:** proceed *only if* CAC < break-even (margin × 1.0). If margin is $30, kill any ad set above ~$30 CAC.
- Days 6–14: release up to $4,500 more into the winning angle **only if the gate passes.** Otherwise, reallocate to influencer seeding.

---

## 3. Launch Channels (3 committed + 1 opportunistic)

Ranked and capped at 3 to protect two-person bandwidth. Each has **one measurable success criterion.**

### Channel 1 — Owned Email/SMS (highest ROI, lowest cost)
- **What:** Warm-up sequence to existing list → launch announcement → 2 follow-ups (Day 3, Day 10).
- **Owner:** Person A.
- **Success criterion:** **≥6% conversion rate** from launch email to purchase (or ≥25% click-to-landing if list is small/cold).
- **"No List" branch:** If list is under ~500, this channel becomes **waitlist-building** instead. Success criterion flips to **≥750 new email/SMS signups** captured via landing page + influencer traffic during the 14 days.

### Channel 2 — Influencer Seeding (the load-bearing channel)
- **What:** Seed ~30 micro-influencers (booktok, cozy home, WFH/desk-setup, 5k–50k followers) with product + a tight brief showing the on-camera warm/cool shift. Small flat fees for the strongest 6–8.
- **Owner:** Person B (sourcing, shipping, briefing, tracking, chasing).
- **Lead time note:** Outreach starts Day 1; content lands Days 6–12. Ship samples by Day 2.
- **Success criterion:** **≥12 pieces of published content** driving **≥400 tracked link clicks** (unique UTM/discount code per creator).

### Channel 3 — Paid Social (gated test — Meta/TikTok)
- **What:** Test 3 message angles Days 1–4; scale winner Days 6–14 *only if gate passes.*
- **Owner:** Person A (must have basic paid-ads competence; if neither person does, cut this channel and reallocate to influencer + waitlist).
- **Success criterion:** **CAC at or below break-even (margin × 1.0)** on the scaled ad set by Day 12. If not met, paid is judged a failed test — that is a valid, documented outcome.

### Opportunistic — PR / Press (no committed target)
- **What:** Best-effort pitches to gadget/home blogs and design newsletters. Personalized, low-volume.
- **Owner:** Shared, in spare cycles only.
- **Not counted toward success.** Any coverage is upside.

---

## 4. Two-Week Timeline (executable by two people)

**Roles:** Person A = Email + Paid + landing page. Person B = Influencer + fulfillment + PR.

### Day 0 (Setup Day — gating)
- Resolve the 3 blocking questions: **margin, inventory status, list size.** (Both)
- Set break-even CAC kill-threshold in writing. (A)
- Confirm sales channel (Shopify recommended — clean UTMs; avoid Amazon's ~15% fee + broken attribution for a launch test). (A)

### Week 1 — Prep, Seed, Test
| Day | Person A (Email/Paid/Site) | Person B (Influencer/PR/Fulfillment) |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Build landing page + purchase flow; set up 3 paid message-test ad sets | Draft brief; identify 30 influencers; begin outreach |
| 2 | Launch $1,500 paid test (angles A/B/C) | Ship samples to first responders; assign unique codes |
| 3 | Email #1 (announce / waitlist); check paid early data | Continue outreach; confirm content dates |
| 4 | Monitor paid; prep Day-5 gate analysis | Chase confirmations; ship remaining samples |
| 5 | **PAID GATE DECISION** — pass = scale winner; fail = reallocate | Send briefs to confirmed creators; begin PR pitches |
| 6 | Scale winning ad angle (if gated) | First influencer content goes live; track clicks |
| 7 | Mid-launch metrics review (both); rebalance budget | Review creator performance; nudge slow creators |

### Week 2 — Amplify, Convert, Learn
| Day | Person A | Person B |
|---|---|---|
| 8 | Optimize paid creative/audiences | More content live; repost/amplify best pieces |
| 9 | Retargeting ad set for landing-page visitors | Continue seeding coverage; PR follow-ups |
| 10 | Email #2 (social proof / creator content) | Collect UGC + reviews from creators |
| 11 | Monitor CAC vs. threshold daily | Push top creators for a second post |
| 12 | **Final paid efficiency check** vs. success criterion | Confirm content total vs. 12-piece target |
| 13 | Email #3 (urgency / last-call) | Wind down outreach; gather testimonials |
| 14 | **Wrap-up + results report** (both): metrics vs. each success criterion, learnings, go/no-go for scaled Phase 2 | |

---

## 5. Top Three Risks & Mitigations

### Risk 1 — Paid spend burns cash below break-even
**Highest financial-damage risk.** At $79 with a physical product, if margin is ~$25–30/unit and cold-social CAC runs $30–50, every paid sale *loses money*. A $25k budget disappears fast.
- **Mitigation:** Confirm margin Day 0. Set a hard break-even CAC kill-threshold. Cap paid at 24% of budget, released in stages, and gated on Day-5 data. Keep influencer + email as load-bearing so the launch survives even if paid fails entirely.

### Risk 2 — Empty funnel / no audience
If the email list is tiny and influencer relationships don't exist, the two cheapest high-ROI channels collapse and everything falls onto paid — triggering Risk 1.
- **Mitigation:** Audit assets Day 0. If no list, flip Channel 1 to **waitlist-building** (target ≥750 signups) and shift weight to influencer seeding. Lower revenue targets to realistic first-launch levels; treat the launch as audience-building.

### Risk 3 — Positioning/claim tension undermines conversion
The differentiator is a wellness benefit we can't fully claim. Too cautious → no reason to pick Lumen over a $40 Hue → low CTR/CVR → high CAC (back to Risk 1). Too aggressive → FTC risk + refund backlash.
- **Mitigation:** Lead with demonstrable, non-medical value (on-camera warm/cool shift, eye comfort, "no harsh light at night," zero-setup automation). Test 3 angles cheaply Days 1–4 before scaling. Scale only the proven winner.

**(Conditional) If launch is pre-order, not in-stock:** Inventory becomes the *top* risk. Success criteria shift from revenue to **refundable deposits / waitlist signups**, and delay/refund risk replaces paid efficiency as risk #1. Mitigation: publish a clear ship-date commitment, take refundable deposits only, and cap paid spend harder until fulfillment is de-risked.

---

## 6. Success Criteria Summary (one per channel)

| Channel | Measurable success criterion |
|---|---|
| Email/SMS | ≥6% email-to-purchase conversion (or ≥750 waitlist signups if no list) |
| Influencer seeding | ≥12 published pieces driving ≥400 tracked clic

# Lumen Launch Plan

**Product:** Lumen — a $79 smart reading lamp that automatically adjusts color temperature to the time of day
**Team:** 2 people (referred to as *Operator A* and *Operator B*)
**Budget:** $25,000 total
**Window:** 14 days (Day 1 = prep begins; public launch = Day 5)

---

## 0. Read This First — Five Decisions That Gate the Plan

This plan is executable as written **only if** the answers below match. If any differ, use the adjustment noted. Confirm all five before spending a dollar.

| # | Question | Plan assumes | If different → adjust |
|---|----------|-------------|----------------------|
| 1 | Is color-temp adjustment **automatic** or app-controlled? | **Automatic (set-and-forget)** — this is the entire differentiator | If app-controlled, drop "automatic" from all copy; reposition around "reading-optimized presets" and lower ad spend by 40% (you're now competing with Hue/Yeelight at a worse price) |
| 2 | Is inventory **in stock** or pre-order? | **In stock, ≥400 units** | If pre-order, add "Ships [date]" to every page/ad, cap orders at inventory, hold 3% budget for refund exposure |
| 3 | Do you have an **existing email list / following**? | **Small or none (<500)** | If list >2,000, promote email to primary launch-day channel and reduce cold paid spend by $2,000 |
| 4 | **True COGS** including shipping + 3% processing + returns? | **~$35–49 contributable margin/unit** | If margin <$30, this plan loses money on paid — pause paid, go bundle/organic only |
| 5 | Is the ad account **aged** (>3 months history)? | **Aged with some spend history** | If brand-new, ramp spend gradually (max $500/day week 1) to avoid a flag/ban |

**One-line truth:** the biggest risk is spending $25k acquiring customers *at a loss* for a *review-less* product. Everything below is built to prevent that.

---

## 1. Positioning

**Lean lifestyle, not clinical.** Avoid health/sleep claims (FTC exposure, weak proof). Sell the feeling.

- **One-liner:** *"The reading lamp that knows what time it is."*
- **Core promise:** Warm, easy-on-the-eyes light at night; crisp, focused light by day — automatically, without an app or a fiddle.
- **Primary audience:** Avid readers and home-office workers who buy for themselves or as a thoughtful $79 gift.
- **Why it wins:** Competitors require app tinkering; Lumen just *does the right thing*. That's the whole story.
- **Proof strategy (critical — we launch with zero reviews):** manufacture social proof *before* paid traffic arrives (see Channel 2 + Timeline Week 1). No cold ad dollars hit a review-less page.

---

## 2. Launch Channels (4 total — deliberately narrow)

Six channels for two people in two weeks fails. We run **three core channels + one launch-day moment.** Reddit and broad PR are cut (self-promo ban risk, low ROI, high coordination cost for two people).

### Channel A — Landing Page + Email Capture *(the money-recovery engine)*
- **Budget:** $1,500 (page build, tools, copy)
- **Role:** Single conversion destination. 97–99% won't buy on first touch — email capture + retargeting is how we recover margin. Includes bundle offers (2-pack, "gift bundle") to lift AOV above CAC.
- **Owner:** Operator A
- **Success criterion (measurable):** ≥25% of unique landing-page visitors submit email OR complete purchase.

### Channel B — Influencer / Micro-Creator Seeding *(social proof + reach)*
- **Budget:** $4,000 (free units + small flat fees to ~15–20 book/home/office micro-creators, 5k–50k followers)
- **Role:** Double duty — reach *and* manufacturing the reviews/UGC our page lacks. Seed **hard in Week 1, before paid ramps.**
- **Owner:** Operator B
- **Success criterion:** ≥10 pieces of published UGC and ≥8 authentic reviews/testimonials live on the site before Day 8.

### Channel C — Paid Social (Meta/Instagram) *(scale — treated as a launch moment, not a verdict)*
- **Budget:** $13,000 total, **front-loaded but with reserve** (see below)
- **Reality check:** Meta needs ~50 conversions per ad set (3–7 days) to exit learning. In 14 days you get ~7–9 days of live spend. **We are not judging channel viability in 14 days — we are launching and building a retargetable audience.**
- **Structure:**
  - Run **2 near-final ad sets from Day 5** (broad + interest), not a wide test grid — speed over perfect optimization given the window.
  - **Spend cap:** $1,200/day max (lower if ad account is new — see Decision #5).
  - **Reserve $3,000** of the $13k to scale the single best-performing ad/audience in Days 12–14.
- **Owner:** Operator A (creative + budget), Operator B (audiences + retargeting)
- **Success criterion:** Achieve blended **ROAS ≥ 1.5x by Day 12** on a rolling 3-day basis (informs post-launch scale/kill decision — not a launch failure if not hit, but a stop-spending trigger if <1.0x by Day 10).

### Channel D — Product Hunt *(one-day event, not a channel)*
- **Budget:** $500 (assets, hunter outreach)
- **Role:** One concentrated spike + credibility badge. Warm the seeded creators + any email list to upvote/comment at launch.
- **Owner:** Operator B
- **Success criterion:** Top 5 Product of the Day OR ≥300 upvotes.

**Budget summary:**

| Line | Amount |
|------|--------|
| Landing page + tools | $1,500 |
| Influencer seeding | $4,000 |
| Paid social (incl. $3k reserve) | $13,000 |
| Product Hunt | $500 |
| Retargeting / email flows | $2,000 |
| Contingency (CS, refunds, defects) | $4,000 |
| **Total** | **$25,000** |

---

## 3. Two-Week Timeline

**Week 1 = prep + seeding + proof (no cold paid spend). Week 2 = launch + scale.**

### Week 1 — Build & Seed

| Day | Operator A (Page / Paid) | Operator B (Creators / PH / CS) |
|-----|--------------------------|--------------------------------|
| 1 | Confirm 5 gating decisions; finalize positioning + copy | Build creator list (15–20 micro-creators); draft seeding pitch |
| 2 | Build landing page (bundles + email capture) | Ship free units to creators; brief them (post by Day 7) |
| 3 | Set up Meta account, pixel, email flows, retargeting audiences | Recruit Product Hunt hunter; prep PH assets |
| 4 | Load 2 ad sets (paused); QA checkout end-to-end | Collect first UGC; draft "loved by" social-proof section |
| 5 | **Soft launch** — page live, retargeting on, low paid ($400) | Publish first creator posts; add reviews to site |
| 6 | Optimize page from real behavior; scale paid to $800/day | Chase remaining creator posts; set up CS templates |
| 7 | Confirm ≥8 reviews live before ramp | Finalize PH launch for Day 8 |

### Week 2 — Launch & Scale

| Day | Operator A | Operator B |
|-----|-----------|-----------|
| 8 | **Product Hunt launch day** — paid steady $1,000; support PH traffic | Run PH all day; rally creators/list to upvote/comment |
| 9 | Review ad data; pause weakest ad; hold cap $1,000 | First-line customer service; log FAQs |
| 10 | **Decision gate:** ROAS <1.0x → cut paid to retargeting only | Push more UGC; email non-purchasers |
| 11 | Scale best ad set toward $1,200/day | CS + gather reviews from early buyers |
| 12 | **Deploy $3k reserve** into single best performer | Send email offer to captured leads |
| 13 | Optimize retargeting; prep final push | CS; publish new buyer reviews |
| 14 | Final push; compile results vs. all success criteria | Handoff doc: what to keep running post-launch |

---

## 4. Top Three Risks & Mitigations

### Risk #1 — Unit economics can't support paid acquisition *(most likely failure mode)*
The $44–54 gross margin drops to ~$35–49 after shipping, ~3% processing, and returns. On a cold, review-less launch, blended CAC above that is *normal*. The realistic bad outcome: ads run "fine" at 1.5x ROAS but you still lose money per sale.
**Mitigations:**
- Bundles (2-pack, gift bundle) on the landing page to lift AOV above CAC.
- Email capture + retargeting to monetize the 97–99% who don't buy first-touch — that's where margin is recovered.
- Hard rule: if 3-day rolling ROAS <1.0x by Day 10, cut cold paid, keep only retargeting.
- Hold $3k reserve to scale only the *proven* winner, not the whole learning-phase burn.

### Risk #2 — Zero reviews / no social proof at launch
A new $79 smart product with no reviews faces brutal conversion friction — often a bigger killer than creative quality.
**Mitigations:**
- Seed creators in Week 1 (Days 1–4) so UGC and ≥8 reviews are live *before* cold paid hits the page (Day 8+).
- Visible "loved by" section on the landing page using micro-creator content.
- Email early buyers for reviews on Days 11–13 to compound proof through the launch.

### Risk #3 — Two-person bandwidth is a single point of failure
A 14-day sprint with paid optimization, PH, creator wrangling, *and* hardware customer service (setup questions, defects, shipping) can halt if one person is out.
**Mitigations:**
- Only 4 channels; Reddit and broad PR cut to protect bandwidth.
- CS templates and an FAQ built by Day 6, before order volume spikes.
- $4k contingency covers CS overflow, defect replacements, and refunds.
- Cross-documented access (accounts, passwords, ad manager) so either person can cover the other.

*Regulatory/claims risk is handled by discipline, not crisis budget: lifestyle copy only, no sleep/health claims — one copy review before anything goes live.*

---

## 5. Definition of Success (roll-up)

- **Landing page:** ≥25% visitor → email or purchase
- **Influencer seeding:** ≥10 UGC pieces + ≥8 live reviews before Day 8
- **Paid social:** blended ROAS ≥1.5x by Day 12 (stop-loss at <1.0x by Day 10)
- **Product Hunt:** Top 5 Product of the Day or ≥300 upvotes

**The 14 days is a launch moment, not a final verdict.** The lasting assets — an email list, retargeting audiences, and a stock of reviews — are what make month two profitable.

# Lumen Launch Plan

**Product:** Lumen — a $79 smart reading lamp that automatically shifts color temperature to match the time of day (energizing cool light in the morning, warm low-blue light at night).
**Team:** 2 people (referred to as **Lead** and **Ops**).
**Total budget:** $25,000
**Window:** 14 days, structured as **Week 1 = build + seed (soft launch), Week 2 = amplify (public launch)**.

---

## 0. Stated Assumptions (confirm on Day 0; the plan branches if any are false)

This plan is only viable if the following hold. Confirm each before spending. Where unknown, the assumed value is stated and used.

| # | Assumption | Value used | If false → |
|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | Gross margin per unit is known | **COGS ≤ $30 → contribution margin ≈ $40/unit after payment fees & shipping** | If margin < $30, cut paid budget by half and lean on organic/PH. |
| A2 | Inventory in stock | **≥ 750 units available now** | If < 300 units, switch to waitlist/pre-order only (see Risk 2). |
| A3 | Fulfillment | **3PL or dropship in place — the two-person team does NOT pack boxes** | If self-fulfilled, block 2 hrs/day per person for packing and reduce all volume targets 30%. |
| A4 | Landing page / store | **Shopify (or equivalent) store exists; only needs a product page refresh** | If no store, spend Days 1–3 building it; drop the referral push. |
| A5 | Email list | **Small list (≈1,500) exists** | If zero, email becomes a *build* channel, not a launch channel. |

**Break-even CPA (from A1):** ~$40. **Paid target CPA: ≤ $25** to preserve margin for returns and overhead.

---

## 1. Positioning

**One angle. Do not dilute.**

> **"The lamp that knows when to help you focus — and when to let you wind down. Automatically."**

- **Primary audience:** Remote workers / knowledge workers who read and work at a home desk into the evening (sleep-quality + eye-comfort motivated).
- **Secondary (upside only):** Avid readers / bibliophiles.
- **The proof:** A single **before/after GIF** showing the lamp shifting from cool daylight (9am) to warm amber (10pm) with no touch. Visual, demoable, undeniable.
- **Price justification (critical):** Lumen sits between $12 smart bulbs and $150+ premium lamps. We win on **experience vs. budget** ("bulbs don't do this, and they look like a science project") and **price vs. premium** ("half the cost of a Casper Glow / Dyson"). Every ad and page must answer: *why $79 and not $19?* → **automatic, hands-free, purpose-built for reading.**

**Copy guardrail:** No medical/health claims ("reduces blue light," "improves sleep," "protects eyes" are OK as comfort/experience language; do NOT claim clinical outcomes).

---

## 2. Budget Allocation ($25,000)

| Line item | Amount | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Paid social (Meta/Instagram) | **$11,000** | Testing budget first, then scale winning creative. Not a volume engine. |
| Influencer / creator seeding | **$5,000** | 8–12 micro-creators (10–80k), product + flat $200–500 fee. |
| Creative production (GIF, photo, 2 video cuts) | **$2,500** | Outsourced editor; assets reused across all channels. |
| PR / Product Hunt prep + tools | **$1,500** | PH hunter outreach, press kit, media list tool. |
| Email/landing tools + referral app | **$1,000** | |
| **Contingency (returns, overspend, restock ads)** | **$4,000** | Do not touch until Day 8. |
| **Total** | **$25,000** | |

---

## 3. Launch Channels + Success Criteria

Each channel has **one measurable success criterion.** Channels are **sequenced, not concurrent**, to fit two people.

### Channel 1 — Email / Waitlist (owned; runs Days 1–14)
- Announce to existing list Day 1, launch offer Day 8, last-call Day 13.
- **Success criterion:** **≥ 8% conversion rate** from launch email to purchase (of clicks), and **≥ 100 orders** attributable to email over the window.

### Channel 2 — Influencer / Creator Seeding (Days 1–9; results land Week 2)
- Ship to 10 creators Day 1–2; posts go live Days 7–10 to stack with public launch.
- Treated as **upside**, not load-bearing.
- **Success criterion:** **≥ 6 of 10 creators post**, generating **≥ 40 tracked-link orders** (unique discount codes).

### Channel 3 — Paid Social (Meta) — testing Days 4–7, scaling Days 8–14
- Test 4 creative variants at $150/day; kill losers; scale the winner in Week 2.
- **Success criterion:** **CPA ≤ $25** on the scaled creative (blended target ≤ $30 including test spend).

### Channel 4 — Product Hunt (single event: Day 11)
- Full-team, all-hands day. Everything else runs on autopilot that day.
- **Success criterion:** **Top 5 Product of the Day** and **≥ 75 orders** on launch day.

> **Sequencing rationale:** PH (free spike, non-repeatable, all-hands) is deliberately placed *after* paid creative is validated and creator posts are live, so the day compounds rather than competes for the team's attention.

---

## 4. Two-Week Timeline

### Week 1 — Build & Seed (Soft Launch)

| Day | Lead | Ops |
|---|---|---|
| **1 (Mon)** | Confirm assumptions A1–A5. Finalize positioning + copy. Send list announcement. | Ship product to 10 creators; assign unique codes. |
| **2 (Tue)** | Brief creative editor (GIF + 2 video cuts). | Product page refresh; set up referral app. |
| **3 (Wed)** | Approve creative v1. Build Meta campaign structure. | Draft PH assets + press kit; line up PH hunter. |
| **4 (Thu)** | **Launch paid tests** (4 variants, $150/day). | Follow up with creators; confirm post dates (Days 7–10). |
| **5 (Fri)** | Review paid data; pause underperformers. | Prep launch email #2; QA checkout & shipping flow. |
| **6–7 (Wk-end)** | Monitor paid; identify winning creative. | Confirm PH launch checklist; nudge creator posts live. |

### Week 2 — Amplify (Public Launch)

| Day | Lead | Ops |
|---|---|---|
| **8 (Mon)** | **Send launch offer email.** Scale winning paid creative. | Coordinate creator posts going live; amplify via story reshares. |
| **9 (Tue)** | Optimize scaled paid; watch CPA vs. $25 cap. | Customer support + review requests to Week-1 buyers. |
| **10 (Wed)** | Refresh creative if fatigue appears. | Final PH prep: teaser, comments ready, thumbnail. |
| **11 (Thu)** | **PRODUCT HUNT LAUNCH — all-hands.** Engage comments all day. | Rally email list + creators to PH; monitor stock. |
| **12 (Fri)** | Post-PH: retarget PH visitors with paid. | Reply to PH, fulfill/monitor 3PL orders. |
| **13 (Sat)** | **Last-call email** (scarcity/ship-date). | Support + collect testimonials. |
| **14 (Sun)** | Compile results vs. all 4 success criteria. Decide continue/cut for each channel. | Reconcile inventory & spend; flag restock needs. |

---

## 5. Top Three Risks & Mitigations

### Risk 1 — Unit economics don't survive paid acquisition *(highest likelihood × impact)*
A $79 product with ~$40 margin and a $20–50 CPA can produce **negative contribution per sale**; scaling ads then loses money faster.
- **Mitigation:** Establish **break-even CPA (~$40) before spending a dollar**; set a hard target of **≤ $25** and daily caps of $150 during testing. Sequence **owned + earned channels first** (email, creators, PH) to lower blended CAC, then use paid only to **amplify proven creative**. Treat paid as a *testing budget, not a volume engine*. Kill any creative above the CPA cap within 48 hours.

### Risk 2 — Inventory / fulfillment mismatch *(binary, existential momentum-killer)*
A PH or creator spike into a stockout wastes the one non-repeatable launch moment; and two people cannot both run a launch and pack boxes.
- **Mitigation:** Confirm stock (A2) and fulfillment method (A3) **on Day 0**. **Cap purchasable quantity to known stock.** If stock < 300, convert the launch to a **pre-order/waitlist** — turning demand risk into a demand *signal* with honest ship dates. Ensure fulfillment is **3PL/dropship** so team bandwidth stays on the launch, never on packing.

### Risk 3 — Weak differentiation at $79 *(medium-high; addressable by execution)*
Buyers may understand auto-color-temp but not value it at $79 when a $12 bulb is "good enough."
- **Mitigation:** Lead with **one sharp angle + the before/after GIF** as visual proof — do not spread across multiple angles. **Seed social proof early:** request reviews/testimonials from Week-1 creator recipients and soft-launch buyers so the PH and paid pushes land against a base of reviews, not zero. Frame price explicitly against both budget and premium alternatives in every asset.

---

## 6. Go / No-Go Gates

- **Day 0:** If A1 (margin) unknown or < $30 → cut paid budget to $5.5K, reallocate to creators/PH. If A2 (stock) < 300 → switch to pre-order.
- **Day 7:** If no paid creative is under $30 CPA → do **not** scale; rely on PH + email + creators only, hold paid at test level.
- **Day 11:** If stock projected to sell out before Day 14 → cap quantities and switch messaging to "final units."

**Provisional note:** All sales and CPA targets are provisional until margin (A1) and inventory (A2) are confirmed on Day 0. Confirm both before releasing any budget.

# Lumen Launch Plan
### $79 Smart Reading Lamp · Two-Person Team · $25,000 Marketing/Ops Budget

---

## 0. Go / No-Go Preconditions (Confirm Before Day 1)

This is a 14-day **launch** plan, not a manufacturing plan. It only works if the physical product is already moving. Do not start Day 1 until all three are true:

1. **Manufacturing is complete.** Finished Lumen units exist.
2. **Inventory is in transit and will land at the 3PL by Day 6** (buffer built in vs. the old Day 8 assumption). Ocean freight is weeks — if units are not already shipping on Day 0, stop and reschedule the launch.
3. **Inventory is customer-financed via pre-order.** The $25,000 is a marketing/ops budget only. It does **not** buy stock. First production run is capped at **300 units**, sold as pre-orders, with a stated fulfillment window disclosed at checkout (see Risk 2).

**Unit economics (must be confirmed with supplier before Day 1):**
- Price: $79
- Assumed landed COGS: ~$45–50/unit
- Payment fees: ~$2.60/unit (2.9% + $0.30)
- Fulfillment/shipping: ~$6/unit
- **Contribution margin ≈ $20–25/unit**

Because contribution margin is ~$20–25, **paid CAC cannot exceed ~$20** or we buy customers at a loss. The CAC ceiling below is derived from this, not chosen arbitrarily.

---

## 1. Positioning

**One line:** *Lumen is the $79 reading lamp that automatically shifts its light to match the time of day — energizing white in the morning, warm amber at night — so your eyes and sleep aren't fighting your lamp.*

**Target buyer:** Home-office workers, readers, and sleep-conscious adults (28–45) who already care about screen time, circadian rhythm, and desk aesthetics.

**Category framing:** Not "another smart bulb." A **purpose-built reading lamp with automatic circadian color temperature** — no app-fiddling required. Set it and forget it.

**Three proof points:**
1. Automatic time-of-day color shift (warm at night, cool by day).
2. $79 — priced below smart-lighting systems, above dumb desk lamps.
3. Designed for reading specifically (glare-free, focused throw).

**Primary objection to defeat:** "Why not just a smart bulb?" → Because a bulb requires setup, an app, and manual scenes. Lumen does it automatically, out of the box.

---

## 2. Budget — Rebuilt to $25,000 (Every Timeline Cost Is a Line Item)

Every dollar named anywhere in this plan appears here. Nothing is hidden in a "hub" bucket.

| Line item | Cost | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Landing/pre-order page build + copy | $1,200 | Shopify theme + custom sections, our labor + small dev help |
| Photography / renders | $1,800 | Explicit line, not absorbed elsewhere |
| Tools & subscriptions (Shopify, apps, email tool) | $600 | ~$150–200/mo × launch period |
| Payment processing fees | $700 | 2.9% + $0.30 on ~300 pre-orders |
| 3PL pick/pack setup + first run | $2,000 | Its own line |
| Sample/defect units for shake test | $150 | ~15 units at COGS |
| Meta ads — validation test | $600 | Days 2–4, waitlist/interest test |
| Meta ads — scale | $8,400 | Primary paid channel, paid-only CAC tracked |
| Influencer seeding (product units + shipping) | $1,500 | ~30 creators × unit+ship |
| Influencer/community paid boost test | $500 | Day 11 |
| Product Hunt / Reddit assets + community | $550 | Assets, moderation tools |
| **Reserve (genuinely unspent)** | **$5,000** | Real contingency, not pre-committed |
| **TOTAL** | **$25,000** | |

**What changed from earlier drafts:** Photography, 3PL, tools, and fees are now their own lines. Meta scale was trimmed from ~$10K to $8.4K to make room. The reserve is real — $5,000 sits untouched.

---

## 3. Launch Channels + One Measurable KPI Each

Attribution reality is baked into every KPI. Reddit strips referrers (shows as "direct"); influencer last-click UTM undercounts assists. Targets are set on what we can actually measure.

| Channel | Owner | Role | **Measurable Success Criterion (one, clean)** |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Landing / Pre-order Page** | Person A | Conversion engine | **≥ 2.5% visitor → pre-order** (single truth — matches gate; 4% was aggressive for $79 hardware, 1.5% too soft) |
| **Meta Ads** | Person A | Paid acquisition | **Paid-only CAC ≤ $20** (not "blended" — blended hides whether Meta works; ceiling derived from ~$20–25 margin) |
| **Influencer Seeding** | Person B | Awareness + social proof | **≥ 12 published posts by Day 14 with ≥ 250 tracked UTM clicks** (dropped the "≥40 attributed pre-orders" target — last-click undercounts creators and would falsely flag a working channel as failed) |
| **Product Hunt + Reddit** | Person B | Earned launch spike | **≥ 1,000 combined referral + coupon-code redemptions** (using a launch-specific discount code as the true attribution signal, since Reddit referrer data is stripped and undercounts badly) |

**Note on Product Hunt:** "Top 5 Product of the Day" is a stretch *outcome*, not a target — it's not controllable. We manage to referral traffic and code redemptions, which we can influence.

---

## 4. Two-Week Timeline (De-Conflicted for Two People)

**Lane split:** Person A = product/ops/paid. Person B = earned/community/content. Launch is **staggered** so PH and Reddit don't collide with support on the same day.

### Week 1 — Build & Validate

| Day | Person A (Ops/Paid) | Person B (Earned/Content) |
|---|---|---|
| **1** | Confirm inventory ETA + landed COGS with supplier. **Start** 3PL onboarding (multi-day process — kick off now, don't expect same-day close). Stand up Shopify + tax/shipping config. | Draft positioning copy, page copy, launch announcement. Build creator target list (~30). |
| **2** | Publish landing page skeleton. Launch **$600 Meta validation test** (waitlist/interest). | Shoot/receive **photography ($1,800)**. Write PH + Reddit assets. |
| **3** | Confirm inventory **in transit**, ETA to 3PL by Day 6. Review test-ad early signal. | Begin creator outreach + ship seeding units (start early — gifted posts take 1–3 weeks, not 72 hrs). |
| **4** | Finalize landing page with photography. Kill/scale test creatives. | Ship remaining seeding units. Confirm PH launch slot. |
| **5** | 3PL integration test (dummy order end-to-end). | Pre-write ALL launch-day content (PH post, Reddit posts, emails) so launch day is monitoring, not creating. |
| **6** | **GATE: Inventory received at 3PL.** If not landed → hold launch. | Schedule waitlist announcement email. Follow up with creators. |
| **7** | Soft QA on checkout + pre-order cap logic (300 units). | First creator posts should begin appearing; track UTM clicks. |

### Week 2 — Launch (Staggered)

| Day | Person A (Ops/Paid) | Person B (Earned/Content) |
|---|---|---|
| **8** | **GATE: Test CAC & page conversion reviewed.** If test paid-CAC trending > $20 or page < 2.5%, fix before scaling. | Final creator nudges. Prep Reddit AMA. |
| **9** | Open pre-orders (soft). Begin Meta scale spend. Monitor fulfillment queue. | Post to owned channels + waitlist warm-up. |
| **10** | **PRODUCT HUNT LAUNCH DAY.** Person A runs support queue + fulfillment. | Person B runs PH all day (full-day job) — comments, engagement. **No Reddit today.** |
| **11** | Monitor Meta scale + paid CAC. Support. | **REDDIT LAUNCH + AMA** (separate day = real-time moderation possible). Run **$500 boost test.** |
| **12** | Fulfillment status check. Send pre-order confirmations/ETAs. | Engage residual PH/Reddit threads. Collect creator post links. |
| **13** | Reconcile spend vs. budget. Paid CAC readout. | Tally creator posts + UTM clicks + code redemptions. |
| **14** | **RETRO** using code redemptions + paid-only CAC + conversion %. Decide on reserve deployment. | Compile earned-channel results honestly (acknowledge Reddit undercount). |

---

## 5. Top Three Risks + Mitigations

### Risk 1 — Inventory doesn't land in time (kills the whole launch)
The 14-day window is fiction if manufacturing isn't done and units aren't in transit on Day 0.
- **Mitigation:** Day-0 go/no-go precondition (Section 0). Day 6 hard gate — if units aren't at the 3PL, launch slips; do not open pre-orders against phantom stock. Pre-orders are explicitly customer-financed with a disclosed fulfillment window, so a short delay doesn't break trust *if disclosed upfront*.

### Risk 2 — Buying customers at a loss (CAC exceeds margin)
Contribution margin is only ~$20–25/unit. A CAC picked at "$25" would erase it.
- **Mitigation:** Paid-only CAC ceiling of **$20**, enforced at the Day 8 gate before scaling. If test CAC trends above $20, do not deploy the $8,400 scale budget — hold in reserve and lean on earned/influencer channels instead. Track paid-only, never "blended."

### Risk 3 — Launch-day overload drops support and trust
The original all-channels-same-day plan was a 3–4 person day for 2 people; support dies during the demand spike.
- **Mitigation:** Staggered launch (PH Day 10, Reddit Day 11). Everything schedulable is pre-written in Week 1, so launch days are monitoring, not creation. On PH day, Person A owns support + fulfillment exclusively while Person B owns PH. Canned support macros for the top 5 expected questions (shipping ETA, warranty, app requirement, returns, order status) written by Day 7.

---

## Summary

The hardware is treated as a real, financed physical object — not free stock on a shelf. Budget sums honestly to $25,000 with a genuine $5,000 reserve. CAC is derived from margin. The timeline gates on physical inventory. Launch is staggered so two humans can actually execute it. KPIs are set on signals we can measure — paid-only CAC, coupon redemptions, tracked UTM clicks — not on undercounting attribution or uncontrollable outcomes like "Top 5 Product of the Day."

# Lumen Launch Plan

---

## 1. Positioning

**One-liner:** *Lumen is the reading lamp that follows your body clock — warm and calm at night, crisp and focused by day, automatically.*

**Target buyer:** Home-office workers, avid readers, and sleep-conscious professionals (28–45) who already invest in their environment (good chairs, coffee, notebooks) but find "smart lighting" either too expensive, too gimmicky, or too complicated.

**Category frame:** Not a "smart home gadget" (crowded, price-anchored to $20 bulbs) but a **focus-and-sleep tool**. We compete on outcome — better reading, easier wind-down — not on lumens or app features.

**Why we win at $79:**
- Automatic circadian color-temp shift without a hub, subscription, or complex setup.
- Priced above impulse-bulb territory (signals quality) but well under premium fixtures ($150+).
- A single, legible benefit: "light that knows what time it is."

**Proof points to lead with:** the automatic day/night transition, one-tap app setup, and a 30-day guarantee.

---

## 2. Launch Channels (with KPIs and budget)

Attribution model is defined up front (see §5) so the Day-8 reallocation runs on clean data.

| Channel | Budget | Owner | Measurable Success Criterion |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Meta / Instagram (paid)** | $8,000 | Person A | **CAC ≤ $35** across 150+ purchases (last-click attributed) |
| **Google Search + Shopping** | $5,000 | Person A | **ROAS ≥ 3.0** |
| **Micro-influencers (gifted + paid)** | $4,800 | Person A | **≥ 50 sales** attributed via unique discount codes |
| **Email / Waitlist + Landing page** | $2,500 | Person B | **1,000+ list, ≥ 8% list-to-purchase conversion** |
| **Payment & platform fees (Shopify + processor + apps)** | $1,200 | Person B | Reconciled separately (see note) |
| **True logistics reserve** | $2,000 | Both | Held; released only on stock-low trigger |
| **Contingency / creative refresh** | $1,500 | Person A | Flexible |
| **Total** | **$25,000** | | |

**Budget notes (audit fixes applied):**
- **The reserve is not double-counted.** The prior draft committed a $2,500 reserve to the Day-8 winning channel *and* claimed the same money as a logistics buffer. Fixed: **$2,000 is a genuine, ring-fenced logistics reserve** (air-freight on a stock-low trigger). The Day-8 reallocation now draws only from the **$1,500 contingency line, capped at $500 of net-new spend** — no phantom money.
- **Payment/platform fees are now a real line ($1,200).** The $2,500 landing/email budget is *net of* transaction costs; the ~3% processor take and app fees are carried separately so they do not silently erode the $35 CAC margin.
- **Influencer fees are realistic.** $4,800 covers 15–20 paid micro-slots at $240–$320 all-in. We assume a **20–30% no-show rate on gifted creators** — this doesn't cost budget (product was gifted) but the 50-sale KPI is set against *delivering* creators only, not gifted headcount.

---

## 3. Two-Week Timeline

**Week 1 — Build & Prime**

| Day | Person A (Demand) | Person B (Ops & Site) |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Draft 3 ad angles; brief editor | **Stand up landing page + waitlist capture** (dedicated 2-day task) |
| 2 | Rough-cut ad creative; short-list influencers | **Finish landing page + install/verify conversion tracking**; define attribution rule (last-click, documented) |
| 3 | Send influencer outreach + gifting; issue unique codes | Set up Shopify, payment processor, inventory alerts, backorder toggle |
| 4 | Finalize Meta + Google campaigns (paused) | Write canned support templates; **model support at 30 tickets/day** (see §5) |
| 5 | Warm waitlist email #1 (teaser); confirm creator commitments | Fulfillment dry-run: pack 5 test units, test tracking emails |

**Week 2 — Launch & Optimize**

| Day | Person A (Demand) | Person B (Ops & Support) |
|---|---|---|
| 6 | **Launch:** Meta + Google live; waitlist email #2 (buy now); influencer posts go live | Fulfillment live; support triage begins |
| 7 | Monitor CAC/ROAS/code sales daily; refresh weakest creative | Ship day-1 orders; monitor inventory vs. 300 cap |
| 8 | **Gate 1 — Reallocation Decision** (rules-based checklist, see §5). Shift up to $500 to widest-beating channel | Confirm stock level; arm logistics reserve if <100 units |
| 9 | Second creative refresh; email #3 to non-openers | Support + fulfillment; toggle backorder if trigger hit |
| 10 | **Gate 2 — Performance Review** against all four KPIs; kill under-performing ad sets | Final inventory reconciliation; publish results snapshot |

---

## 4. Top Three Risks & Mitigations

**Risk 1 — Single-person unavailability during Days 6–9 collapses both gates.**
A two-person team has no bench; if either person is out 48 hours in launch week, demand *and* ops both stall.
**Mitigation:**
- **Cross-train and share access now** (Day 3): documented logins for Meta, Google, Shopify, email, and influencer tracker in a shared vault.
- **The Day-8 reallocation is a written rules-based checklist** (see §5) — *either* person can execute it without judgment calls.
- Pre-write Day 6–10 daily tasks as a runnable checklist so the remaining person can hold the line solo for 48 hours.

**Risk 2 — Zero inventory slack; any channel over-performing triggers a stockout.**
The KPIs sum to ~300 units (150 Meta + 50 influencer + 80 email + Google) — *exactly* the 300-unit inventory cap. There is no margin for over-success.
**Mitigation:**
- **$2,000 genuine logistics reserve** (not double-committed) funds air-freight if stock drops below 100 units on Day 8.
- **Backorder toggle** ready in Shopify: over-demand converts to pre-orders with a clear ship date rather than lost sales or oversells.
- Inventory alert at 100 units flips the reserve from "held" to "spend" automatically.

**Risk 3 — Attribution double-counting misfires the Day-8 reallocation.**
Meta CAC, Google ROAS, influencer codes, and email conversion will all claim the same multi-touch buyers. Without a rule, we could defund the channel actually driving discovery.
**Mitigation:**
- **Attribution rule locked on Day 2: last-click for paid channels; unique discount codes override for influencers.** Documented before any spend goes live.
- Day-8 decision reads only from the single, agreed attribution view — no ad-hoc interpretation.

---

## 5. Operating Details (resolves remaining audit flags)

**Day-8 Reallocation Checklist (rules-based, either person executes):**
1. Pull last-click CAC (Meta), ROAS (Google), code-sales (influencer), list-conversion (email).
2. Identify the channel beating its target by the **widest percentage margin**.
3. If that channel is scalable (Meta/Google), move up to **$500 from the contingency line** into it. No more.
4. If inventory <100 units, **do not scale demand** — arm the logistics reserve instead.
5. Log the decision and timestamp in the shared doc.

**Support model:** Assume **~30 tickets/day** at launch (technical product: app setup, color-temp scheduling). Person B budgets **~2–3 hrs/day** on canned-template support. If tickets exceed 45/day for two consecutive days, Person A absorbs first-line triage and pauses one creative-refresh cycle. Fulfillment and inventory monitoring take priority over new-order packing beyond the daily cutoff.

**Fee reconciliation:** Landing/email budget ($2,500) is net of costs; the $1,200 fee line covers Shopify plan, ~3% processor take on projected revenue, and required apps. CAC ≤$35 target is measured **after** these fees.

---

**Bottom line:** The two gates (Day-8 reallocation, Day-10 performance review) remain the plan's backbone. Budget now reconciles cleanly with a real $2,000 reserve, personnel contingency is documented for the critical window, and the attribution rule is fixed before the first decision gate — making this executable by two people within $25,000.

# Lumen Launch Plan

**Product:** Lumen — a $79 smart reading lamp that automatically adjusts color temperature to the time of day (cool/energizing by day, warm/low-blue by night).
**Team:** 2 people (Person A — Growth/Marketing; Person B — Ops/Product/Support).
**Marketing budget:** $25,000. **This plan is executable in 14 days *only if the two gates below are cleared first.***

---

## ⛔ GO / NO-GO GATES — Clear these before Day 1 or the timeline is invalid

This plan describes a 14-day launch. It is physically impossible unless both of these are true **before** Day 1. If either fails, this becomes a 6–10 week plan.

**GATE 1 — Inventory is already produced and in-warehouse.**
A 330-unit smart-lamp batch (300 for sale + 10% reserve for defects/reviewers/returns) cannot go from purchase order to QA'd-and-shippable in 5 business days. Manufacturing + freight + customs is weeks-to-months. **Confirm in writing that inventory is physically received and QA-passed before publishing this timeline.** Day 1 of this plan is "confirm inventory on hand" — never "place order."

**GATE 2 — Total cash, including COGS, is confirmed to exist.**
The $25k is marketing only. Real product cash is additional:
- COGS at ~$25/unit × 330 units = **~$8,250**, often payable partly upfront.
- **True total cash required: ~$33,250.**

Confirm in writing that the ~$8,250 COGS is funded *separately* from the $25k marketing budget. If $25k is all the money that exists, shrink the batch to ~200 units and rescale sales targets before proceeding.

**A third precondition starts today, before Day 1:**
**Warm up ad accounts now.** New Meta/Google accounts have low spend caps and review holds — you cannot push $8,200 through a 9-day-old account on launch day. Spend ~$50/day on both accounts starting 9+ days before launch to establish spend history and trust.

---

## 1. Positioning

**One-liner:** *Lumen is the reading lamp that protects your evenings — it shifts from focused daylight to warm, low-blue light as the day ends, so you can read late without wrecking your sleep.*

**Target buyer:** Home readers and remote knowledge-workers, 28–55, who already care about sleep hygiene and screen/blue-light exposure, and who read in bed or in a home office at night.

**Category frame:** Not "another smart bulb." Lumen is a *dedicated reading light with automatic circadian adjustment* — no app-fiddling required. The core promise is **"set it once, never think about it."**

**Proof points:** Automatic time-of-day color temperature; single-purpose (reading, not whole-room); $79 (premium over dumb lamps, accessible vs. smart-home ecosystems).

**Why buy now:** Launch-window pricing and limited first-batch availability (330 units).

---

## 2. Launch Channels — with per-channel KPIs

### Channel A — Meta Ads (Person A) — $8,200
- **Role:** Primary paid acquisition + list-building.
- **Attribution:** Meta pixel + platform-reported purchases, cross-checked against Shopify.
- **Success criterion (single, profitability-based):** **ROAS ≥ 3.0x.** At $79 revenue / ~$25 COGS, a ~$26 CAC (3.0x) leaves healthy margin. *Note: a $40 CAC / 2.0x target is break-even, not a win — we do not use it.*

### Channel B — Google Search (Person A) — $2,300
- **Role:** Capture high-intent demand.
- **Success criteria (segmented — branded search would otherwise flatter the channel on ~zero pre-launch volume):**
  - **Non-branded/generic terms** (e.g. "reading lamp for sleep"): **CAC ≤ $45.**
  - **Branded terms** ("Lumen lamp"): treated as brand-defense, target **conversion ≥ 8%**, minimal spend.

### Channel C — Influencer / Creator Seeding (Person B) — $6,000
- **Role:** Awareness + social proof in reading/sleep/home niches.
- **Spend reality:** ~$400/creator × ~13 creators + ~$25 free product each. Target genuinely engaged niche creators, **not** follower-count buys.
- **Attribution (explicitly defined):** Each creator gets a **unique promo code + a unique UTM'd landing-page URL.** We count both. We acknowledge promo codes undercount (some viewers buy later without the code), so we treat attributed sales as a *floor*, not the full effect.
- **Success criterion:** **≥ 13 posts live, and ≥ 90 attributed sales** (code or UTM) across all creators (~7/creator — realistic for engaged niches). Engagement % is tracked but is not a success gate on its own.

### Channel D — Email / Pre-order (Person B) — $700 (tools/Klaviyo)
- **Role:** Convert warmed traffic; capture non-immediate buyers.
- **List assumption stated:** The list is **built by Channels A/B during the launch** — we start from ~0. Target **list of ≥ 1,200 emails by Day 10.**
- **Pre-order legality:** Configure Stripe/Shopify to **charge on ship, not on order** (or clearly disclose ship date at checkout) to stay within consumer-protection rules. Since inventory is in-warehouse (Gate 1), we ship within 48h — this risk is contained.
- **Success criterion:** **≥ 6% email → purchase rate** on the launch flow, and **≥ 200 total orders** in the 14-day window (revised down from an aggressive 300 for a cold-start no-name brand).

---

## 3. Two-Week Timeline (14 days)

*Assumes Gates 1 & 2 cleared and ad accounts already warming since ~Day –9.*

| Day | Person A (Growth) | Person B (Ops/Product/Support) |
|---|---|---|
| **D1** | Confirm ad accounts warmed & caps lifted; build campaign structures | **Confirm inventory on-hand & QA'd**; confirm fulfillment/shipping ready |
| **D2** | Launch $1,000 waitlist/validation ad set (runs D2–D7, 5+ days) | Draft creator shortlist; begin outreach |
| **D3** | Build landing page + Meta creative variants | Send product to first creators; set up Klaviyo flows |
| **D4** | Monitor validation (still in learning phase — no decisions yet) | Finalize creator agreements + unique codes/UTMs |
| **D5** | Draft launch ad copy | **Freelancer starts here** (not launch week) — assist QA + creator logistics |
| **D6** | Split asset writing across BOTH people (see load note) | Confirm creator content ETAs; set up support macros |
| **D7** | **Read validation (5-day signal)** — directional read, not a hard coin-flip gate | Pre-write support/FAQ; test checkout end-to-end |
| **D8** | Finalize campaigns based on validation direction | Confirm all creator posts scheduled |
| **D9** | Final ad account/spend-cap check; stage budgets | Final fulfillment dry run |
| **D10** | **LAUNCH** — Meta + Google live; email announce | Creator posts go live; monitor orders/support |
| **D11** | Optimize by ROAS; kill losers | Fulfill orders; handle support |
| **D12** | Scale winners | Fulfill + support; QA any early returns |
| **D13** | Retargeting push to email list | Fulfill + support |
| **D14** | Report KPIs vs. targets | Report ops/returns; **hand off to standing support plan** |

**Load rebalancing:** Asset-writing (emails, ad copy, FAQ, macros) is split across both people D5–D7 (not dumped on one weekend). Freelancer ($1,500, see budget) starts **Day 5**, not launch week.

**Post-D14 support:** Launch ends; customers don't. A lightweight standing plan (shared inbox, macros from D7, freelancer on-call ~5 hrs/week for 2 weeks) covers post-launch support and returns.

---

## 4. Budget Breakdown ($25,000 marketing)

| Line | Amount | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Meta Ads | $8,200 | Includes $1,000 validation (D2–D7) |
| Google Search | $2,300 | Segmented branded vs. generic |
| Influencer seeding | $6,000 | ~13 creators × ~$400 + free product |
| Ad-account warm-up | $900 | ~$50/day × 9 days, both accounts, pre-launch |
| Email/tools (Klaviyo, landing page) | $700 | |
| Freelancer (support/ops, D5–D14 + 2 wks post) | $2,300 | Extended from launch-week-only |
| Returns/refunds + return shipping | $700 | **New line — a lamp will have returns** |
| Photography/creative production | $1,400 | |
| Contingency / true slack | $2,500 | 10% — this is tight, not comfortable |
| **Total** | **$25,000** | |

**Cash outside this budget (must be separately funded — Gate 2):** ~$8,250 COGS. **True total cash: ~$33,250.**

---

## 5. Top Three Risks & Mitigations

**Risk 1 — Inventory / lead-time collapse.**
The entire 14-day plan sits on inventory being physically in-warehouse. If it's not, nothing after Day 1 is real, and creator seeding (shipping product) fails immediately.
*Mitigation:* Gate 1 — written confirmation of received, QA'd stock before Day 1. If not in hand, publicly reframe as a 6–10 week timeline; do not spend a dollar until confirmed.

**Risk 2 — Cash shortfall (COGS not funded).**
If the $25k is all the money there is, the plan is really ~$33k and does not close.
*Mitigation:* Gate 2 — written confirmation COGS (~$8,250) is funded separately. If not, cut batch to ~200 units and rescale sales targets to ~130 orders before launch.

**Risk 3 — Ad accounts throttled on launch day.**
New Meta/Google accounts have low spend caps and review holds; you cannot push $8,200 through a 9-day-old account.
*Mitigation:* Warm both accounts at ~$50/day starting 9+ days pre-launch ($900 budgeted). If caps aren't lifted by D9, shift launch-day spend toward Google/email and ramp Meta over D10–D14 rather than front-loading.

---

**Bottom line:** Positioning and roles are sound. This plan is executable by two people in 14 days **if and only if** inventory is in-warehouse and COGS is separately funded. Those two items are go/no-go gates, not footnotes — verify both in writing before spending anything.
